nuggets.headline: the beat for 'HUMBLE.' was originally intended for Gucci Mane
nuggets.text: The infectious, stark piano beat that drives 'HUMBLE.' was crafted by producer Mike WiLL Made-It, but he didn't initially envision it for Kendrick Lamar. Mike WiLL Made-It had actually made the beat with Gucci Mane in mind, a surprising intended destination given the final track. However, after Kendrick Lamar heard the beat, he recorded his verses, and ultimately, others convinced him to keep the track for his album *DAMN.* rather than it appearing on Mike WiLL Made-It's own project, *Ransom 2*.

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: "Humble" was the first song to be recorded for Damn. The beat for "Humble" was developed by Mike Will Made It, with the intention of recording the song with Gucci Mane. However, the song was later shown to Lamar. After recording, it was initially agreed upon that it would be released on Mike Will Made's debut album Ransom 2, but others convinced Lamar to keep it for his upcoming album.

nuggets.imageCaption: Mike WiLL Made-It produced 'HUMBLE.' and other tracks on DAMN.

nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar intentionally made *DAMN.* more accessible after 'To Pimp a Butterfly'
nuggets.text: After the critically acclaimed but complex jazz and funk sound of *To Pimp a Butterfly*, Kendrick Lamar approached *DAMN.* with a clear goal: to create a more accessible album. He aimed for a hybrid sound, blending elements from his first two commercial albums, *good kid, m.A.A.d city* and *To Pimp a Butterfly*, focusing on sonic, lyrical, and melodic accessibility. Kendrick revealed he spent a full year conceptualizing and executing this new sound, recognizing that a direct sequel to *good kid, m.A.A.d city* would feel disingenuous.

source.title: Kendrick Lamar Talks DAMN., Ghostwriting, and "Wack Artists" in New Interview - SPIN
source.quoteSnippet: The initial goal was to make a hybrid of my first two commercial albums. That was our total focus, how to do that sonically, lyrically, through melody – and it came out exactly how I heard it in my head. … Going from To Pimp a Butterfly to DAMN., that shit could have crashed and burned if it wasn’t executed right.

nuggets.headline: explore ScHoolboy Q, the artist who connected Kendrick Lamar with 'HUMBLE.' producer Mike WiLL Made-It
nuggets.text: If you're digging the sound of 'HUMBLE.' and want to explore more, check out ScHoolboy Q. He's not just a labelmate on Top Dawg Entertainment; he played a direct role in the creation of *DAMN.*'s sound. It was ScHoolboy Q who first connected Kendrick Lamar with producer Mike WiLL Made-It, who then went on to produce 'HUMBLE.' and other tracks on the album. To start, listen to ScHoolboy Q's track 'My Hatin Joint' from his 2012 album *Habits & Contradictions*, also produced by Mike WiLL Made-It.

source.title: How Mike WiLL Made-It And Kendrick Lamar Created The Year's Most Urgent Music : The Record : NPR
source.quoteSnippet: Me and [K.] Dot been working on this for years, ever since good kid, m.A.A.d city. Schoolboy Q is the one who really hooked us up. Me and Schoolboy were already cool cause I had produced "My Hatin Joint" on his [2012] album Habits and Contradictions.

nuggets.imageCaption: ScHoolboy Q, a fellow TDE artist and early collaborator.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar, the Compton native, stands as one of hip hop's most profound storytellers, known for his cinematic albums that delve into themes of faith, identity, and the Black American experience. Each project marks a distinct evolution, pushing the boundaries of rap while maintaining a deep, intricate narrative structure.
